what_it_does: Parses command-line arguments for an 'episodic-memory search' command, collects query terms and filters, then dispatches to either a single-concept search (vector/text/both) or a multi-concept AND search, printing formatted results.
observations: Imports searchConversations, formatResults, searchMultipleConcepts, formatMultiConceptResults, and SearchOptions from ./search.js.
Default search mode is 'both'; --vector and --text switch to vector-only or text-only.
--source accepts comma-separated or repeated values, accumulated into an array.
All non-flag positional arguments are collected as queries.
Multiple positional queries trigger searchMultipleConcepts; a single query triggers searchConversations.
The multi-concept branch builds an options object that omits the mode field.
standouts: The CLI distinguishes single-query vs multi-query dispatch by arity.
Capture-source filtering uses repeatable/comma-separated accumulation into an IN-list.
Help text advertises a '--both' flag that the argument parser does not explicitly handle.
conventions: Uses process.argv.slice(2) manual argument parsing with an index-advancing loop.
Uses TypeScript type annotations for mode and options.
Maps CLI flag names to snake_case option keys (session_id, git_branch).
Asynchronous search calls use .then/.catch rather than async/await at top level.
concerns: The help example shows '--both', but passing --both would be interpreted as a query term because it is not handled in the parser.
When multiple queries are provided, the selected --vector/--text/--both mode is ignored because searchMultipleConcepts options omit mode.
parseInt for --limit uses no radix and no NaN handling.
No validation that --after/--before values are valid dates.
Manual argv parsing is fragile with edge cases such as missing values after flags.
notes: A thin CLI wrapper that turns argv into search options and picks between single-concept and multi-concept search functions.
questions: Does searchMultipleConcepts internally use vector, text, or both, and why is mode not passed?
What is the intended semantics of capture-sources 'claude | codex | board'?
Are formatResults and formatMultiConceptResults synchronous or asynchronous?
Should --both be a recognized flag, or is the help text outdated?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches for single-query search; the help text's --both example and multi-concept path's omission of mode create small mismatches.
apparent_themes: Arity-based dispatch between single and multi-concept queries
intention_signals: To give users a flexible terminal interface for searching indexed conversations with semantic, exact, and combined matching plus project/time/source/git filters.
novelty: The capture-source IN-list is built by repeatable/comma-separated accumulation, and multi-concept AND search is triggered simply by providing multiple positional query strings.
contribution: Provides the argv-to-search translation layer, help documentation, mode selection, filter assembly, and the dispatch logic that routes single and multiple query inputs to the appropriate search function.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a TypeScript CLI entry point that parses arguments for an episodic-memory search command, assembles filters and query terms, and dispatches to single-concept or multi-concept search functions from ./search.js.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to expose the underlying search library through a user-friendly command-line interface with help, mode selection, and rich filtering options.
